overview: Today's pre-market main storyline is clear: Middle East tensions lift oil prices, raise inflation and rate expectations, and tech/growth are most sensitive to 'higher for longer' rates. The market performance reflects that: Dow more resilient, Nasdaq weaker. If one sentence: Energy steals the show, rates apply pressure, AI/software chain starts to give back valuations.

headlines.title: Middle East tensions boost oil, energy shock reins in market sentiment
headlines.why: Brent crude up ~+2.9% to around $102.8; Iran conflict and Strait of Hormuz risks repeatedly highlighted. Once oil reclaims triple digits, the market immediately translates it into second-round inflation, affecting Fed path, long-end rates, and growth valuations.
headlines.bullish: Energy stocks, oil services, upstream resources, some defensive cash-flow assets.
headlines.bearish: Airlines, cruises, transportation, discretionary, high-valuation tech.

headlines.title: 10Y Treasury yield rises to ~4.40%, market reprices 'higher/longer'
headlines.why: US 10Y yield ~4.40%, intraday +6.4bp; conflict prolongation brings 2026 rate-hike/tighter credit conditions back into discussion. Rates directly decide market style — value/energy/Dow components relatively stable, longer-duration tech/growth more fragile.
headlines.bullish: Bank net interest margin logic, insurance, some value stocks.
headlines.bearish: Software, semiconductors, high-valuation AI narrative stocks, long-duration assets.

headlines.title: Tech/AI trade松动, software and high-beta AI chain under pressure
headlines.why: MarketWatch/Barron’s pre-market: 'AI jitters return', software weak; Super Micro (SMCI) target cut; movers include Tesla, ServiceNow, Gartner. This is not a single-company story; it signals AI trade shifting from 'narrative-only' back to 'valuation + rates + delivery'.
headlines.bullish: Real profit delivery, less crazy mega-cap platform names, defensive cash cows.
headlines.bearish: High-beta AI concepts, server chain with high expectations, high-PS software.

headlines.title: Market not in full panic but 'sector rotation' is obvious
headlines.why: Reuters snapshot: Dow ~+0.14%, S&P ~-0.09%, Nasdaq ~-0.49%. MarketWatch similarly: Dow -0.16%, S&P -0.34%, Nasdaq -0.75%. This indicates not pure risk-off but sector rotation. Money hasn't left entirely; it's moving from growth to energy/defense/value.
headlines.bullish: Dow heavyweights, traditional value, energy, defense sectors.

headlines.title: Wall Street views diverge: some raise S&P target but near-term risks remain
headlines.why: CNBC/MarketWatch note that despite Middle East and credit risks, some big banks raised S&P targets. This shows medium-term funds haven't abandoned the bull framework, but short-term trading focuses more on geopolitics, oil, and rates.
headlines.bullish: If oil falls and yields stabilize, risk-on can resume quickly.
headlines.bearish: If conflict escalates, current 'style rotation' may upgrade to broader pullback.

sectors.AI / Semiconductor: Weak today. Core issue not AI logic collapse but rate hikes hit the most expensive AI narrative stocks first. Expect bounce if 10Y falls. Focus on real earnings names.
sectors.Big Tech: Same logic as AI. Not a company-specific event but macro discount rate issue. When rates stay high, big tech survives on earnings quality; smaller names weaker.
sectors.Energy: Still most direct beneficiary. Watch out: if US/allies really stabilize oil, energy may see 'good news sold'.
sectors.EV: Weak. Not EV-specific but risk-off environment + high rates. Although high oil price theoretically benefits EV demand, market usually front-runs macro pressure, not long-term demand improvement.
sectors.US Market: Currently macro-headline driven, not bottom-up stock picking. S&P/Nasdaq short-term moves will be led by: Middle East news, oil prices, Treasury yields, Friday derivatives expiry.

preMarket: Key numbers to watch: 1) Brent crude (watch for renewed spike); 2) 10Y Treasury yield (direct pressure on AI/growth); 3) US dollar (strength hurts gold and risk assets); 4) Futures recovery sustainability.
verdict: Cautious tone. Not an immediate crash but not suitable for aggressive buying. Expect energy to hold up; growth to watch rates; broad indices high volatility without clean direction. Wait for Middle East de-escalation signals, oil stabilization, and yields to stop climbing before considering stronger risk-on.
